title: Packaging-Portfolio Sizzle Reel: Grade the Stills, Cut the Hero Video
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief: the client is a packaging designer who supplies "mockups and photographs of packaging projects that I have completed," wants an "eye-catching sizzle reel to be used as the hero video on my new website," and wants it "fast paced and energetic while showcasing my design work in the best way possible." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: (1) the count and nature of the supplied stills (6 packaging mockup/product photos shot in mixed studio lighting, plus one bare hero box shot on a generous charcoal sweep so its surround can be solid-filled to a clean card and then reframe-cropped to a wide title plate) is specified so the chain has concrete inputs; (2) the explicit deliverable specs (1920x1080 master + 1080x1080 social cut + a graded still set) are enriched, since the brief only says "hero video"; (3) the cohesive-grade requirement is implied by "showcasing my design work in the best way possible" across photos shot at different times. NOT invented: no new subject matter, no unrelated deliverable — the deliverable is exactly the requested sizzle reel, built honestly from the supplied stills because the connector cannot film or composite. The video-assembly steps are truthfully tagged [A] (async-widget): in the Adobe×Claude product the Quick Cut / resize progress widget polls and notifies; they are real product tools, not headless-retrievable.
one_line_ask: Take my mixed-lighting packaging photos, color-grade them into one cohesive polished hero set, then assemble a fast-paced 16:9 website sizzle reel plus a square social cut.
full_brief:
I run a small packaging design studio and I'm launching a new website. I have a folder of finished work — six photographs/mockups of packaging projects I've shipped (a tea carton, a cosmetics jar, a coffee bag, a craft-beer can, a perfume box, and a chocolate bar sleeve), all shot in my studio over the past year so they don't match: some are warm, some are cool, a couple are slightly crooked, exposure drifts shot to shot. I also have one clean photo of a plain kraft box I want to use as the title card — please seat it on a clean solid studio card so I have even negative space to overlay my logo.

I want all of this pulled together into an eye-catching HERO sizzle reel for the top of my homepage: fast-paced, energetic, and making my design work look its absolute best. Please do the following, in order, so the look is cohesive across every shot:

1) Bring all my supplied photos in and get them straight and uniformly exposed (auto-straighten the crooked ones, auto-tone, lift the dark portions a touch).
2) Give the whole set one consistent grade — neutralize the mixed white balance toward a clean studio look, push vibrance so the print colors pop, apply a single cinematic Lightroom preset across every frame, and add a light film grain so they read as one family.
3) For the title card: seat the kraft box on a solid charcoal studio card so there's clean negative space for my logo, and also make a clean "cut-out" hero variant of the cosmetics jar — remove its background and drop it onto a solid charcoal card.
4) Crop/resize every graded frame to a uniform 1920x1080 so they sequence cleanly.
5) Pull in one licensed studio-texture stock plate to use as the intro/outro background.
6) Assemble the graded frames into a fast, energetic ~20-second sizzle reel at 1920x1080 for the website hero, and also give me a 1080x1080 square cut for Instagram.

Quality bar: every shot must look like it belongs to the same set; print colors must be punchy but not blown out; the title-card plate must be clean with even negative space.

inputs.name: pkg_tea_carton.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product photograph of a printed rectangular tea carton (loose-leaf tea packaging) standing on a seamless light backdrop, warm tungsten studio lighting, slightly warm white balance, box very subtly tilted off-vertical (a few degrees crooked), visible print artwork and fold seams, clean commercial product shot. 3000x2000 landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: One of six real client packaging photos shot in a home studio over a year, deliberately warm-toned and a touch crooked so image_auto_straighten (step 3) and the cohesive-grade chain have honest work to do. No text-to-image compositing needed downstream — used only as a source frame for editing.

inputs.name: pkg_cosmetics_jar.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product photograph of a frosted-glass cosmetics jar with a metal lid on a seamless pale-grey backdrop, cool daylight-balanced studio lighting, slightly cool white balance, crisp reflections, clean commercial product shot with a clean separable background around the jar. 3000x2000 landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: The jar has a clean, evenly-lit separable background so image_remove_background (step 11) can strip it cleanly for the hero cut-out. Cool WB contrasts with the warm tea/perfume shots, giving the WB-neutralize step (6) real spread to correct.

inputs.name: pkg_coffee_bag.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product photograph of a matte foil coffee bag with a printed label and degassing valve, standing on a dark seamless backdrop, moody underexposed studio lighting so shadow detail is slightly crushed, clean commercial product shot. 3000x2000 landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately underexposed (crushed shadows) so image_adjust_dark_portions (step 5) has real dark print detail to recover. One of the six source frames; edit-only, never generated further.

inputs.name: pkg_craft_beer_can.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product photograph of a printed craft-beer aluminum can with vivid label artwork, standing on a seamless neutral backdrop, mixed studio lighting, can subtly tilted a few degrees off-vertical (slightly crooked), condensation highlights, clean commercial product shot. 3000x2000 landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Subtly crooked so image_auto_straighten (step 3) levels it alongside the tea carton. Vivid label gives the vibrance step (7) punchy print color to push without blowing out.

inputs.name: pkg_perfume_box.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product photograph of a luxury perfume box with foil-stamped print detail on a seamless backdrop, warm golden studio lighting, warm white balance, soft reflections, clean commercial product shot. 3000x2000 landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Warm-toned like the tea carton, contrasting the cool jar — part of the mixed-lighting spread the cohesive grade must neutralize. Source frame only; edited in place, never regenerated.

inputs.name: pkg_chocolate_bar_sleeve.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product photograph of a printed chocolate-bar paper sleeve with rich illustrated artwork, laid on a seamless neutral backdrop, even but slightly flat studio lighting, clean commercial product shot. 3000x2000 landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Slightly flat exposure so image_apply_auto_tone (step 4) has contrast to fix. Illustrated artwork benefits from the shared cinematic preset (step 8) and grain (step 9) that bind the six shots into one look.

inputs.name: pkg_kraft_box_titlecard.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio photograph of a plain unbranded kraft cardboard box centered on a seamless charcoal studio sweep, soft even light, real corrugated edge and tape seam, generous empty charcoal sweep around the box, clean commercial product shot, no logo. 3000x3000 near-square.
inputs.realism_notes: Centered on a generous charcoal sweep so image_fill_area (step 10) can solid-fill the surrounding region to a clean charcoal #2B2B2B card (no invented pixels, no canvas growth) and step 13 crops it to the wide 1920x1080 title plate — all gen-free.

outputs.name: packaging_sizzle_hero_16x9.mp4
outputs.spec: Fast-paced energetic sizzle reel, 1920x1080 (16:9), ~20s, assembled from the 6 graded packaging frames + charcoal-seated reframe-cropped title card + stock intro/outro plate via Quick Cut. Website hero use. [A]

outputs.name: packaging_sizzle_social_1x1.mp4
outputs.spec: 1080x1080 (1:1) square reframe of the hero reel, SAME length (video_resize is same-duration), for Instagram feed. [A]

outputs.name: graded_frames_1920x1080/ (6 stills)
outputs.spec: Six cohesively color-graded, straightened, preset+grain-matched packaging stills, each cropped/resized to 1920x1080 JPEG — the frame set the reel is built from. [C]

outputs.name: titlecard_kraft_fullbleed.png
outputs.spec: Kraft-box title card reframe-cropped to a wide 1920x1080 full-bleed plate with clean negative space for logo overlay. [C]

outputs.name: cosmetics_jar_cutout_charcoal.png
outputs.spec: Cosmetics jar with background removed, composited onto a solid charcoal (#2B2B2B) card via solid-fill — clean hero variant. [C]

connector_workflow.output: uploaded_pkg_assets (7 client stills: 6 packaging photos + kraft title card)
connector_workflow.note: Initialize upload of the seven client-supplied stills into CC storage so every downstream image_* tool has a real source URI. Initial asset load — no prior input.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: uploaded_pkg_assets (7 client stills: 6 packaging photos + kraft title card)
connector_workflow.note: Finalize the multipart upload; returns stable asset URIs consumed by the prep chain.
Level the crooked tea-carton and beer-can tilts. Consumes the stored URNs from step 2.
Per-frame auto exposure/contrast baseline across the mixed-lighting set. Consumes step 3 output.
Lift crushed shadows (esp. the underexposed coffee bag) so dark print detail reads. Consumes step 4 output.
Neutralize the warm/cool WB spread (warm tea/perfume vs cool jar) toward one clean studio white point — core of the cohesive grade. Consumes step 5 output.
Push vibrance so printed/illustrated package colors pop without blowing out. Consumes step 6 output.
Apply ONE cinematic Lightroom preset identically to every frame to bind the set into a single look. Consumes step 7 output.
Add a light, uniform film grain across all frames so they read as one family on the reel. Consumes step 8 output.

connector_workflow.output: titlecard_charcoal_seated (kraft box seated on a clean solid charcoal #2B2B2B card within the existing near-square canvas)
connector_workflow.note: Select the charcoal sweep surrounding the kraft box and solid-fill it to a uniform charcoal #2B2B2B, seating the box on a clean studio card with even negative space for the logo (solid color only — no canvas growth, no invented pixels, no compositing). Consumes the graded kraft frame from step 9; step 13 crops this to the wide 1920x1080 title plate.
Remove the cosmetics-jar background from its graded frame to make the clean hero cut-out. Consumes the graded jar frame from step 9.
Solid-fill the now-transparent area with charcoal #2B2B2B to seat the cut-out jar on a clean studio card (solid color only — no compositing). Consumes step 11 output.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: grained_frames, titlecard_charcoal_seated (kraft box seated on a clean solid charcoal #2B2B2B card within the existing near-square canvas), jar_cutout_charcoal (jar on solid #2B2B2B card)
connector_workflow.output: frames_1920x1080 (uniform 16:9 graded frame set + 1920x1080 title plate titlecard_kraft_fullbleed)
connector_workflow.note: Crop/resize every graded frame + the charcoal-seated title card + the cut-out to a uniform 1920x1080 so they sequence cleanly (a pure reframe/crop of existing pixels — no canvas invented, padded, or extended). This is where the wide title plate titlecard_kraft_fullbleed is produced by cropping the near-square seated card to 16:9. Consumes steps 9, 10 and 12.

Search Adobe Stock for a neutral studio-texture plate, license it, and pull the full-res presigned URL for the intro/outro background. License-before-edit. (Independent stock fetch — runs alongside the prep chain.)
Resize the licensed stock plate to 1920x1080 to match the frame set as the intro/outro background. Consumes step 14 output.

Assemble the uniform graded frames + intro/outro plate into a fast, energetic ~20s sizzle reel via Quick Cut (prompt = 'fast-paced energetic packaging showcase'). [A] async-widget: progress widget polls + notifies in the product. Consumes steps 13 and 15.
Reframe the finished hero reel to a 1080x1080 square Instagram cut — SAME duration (video_resize never trims). [A] async-widget. Consumes step 16.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Steps 1-2 load the 7 client stills into CC storage (pkg_asset_urns) — the only non-chained initial load. The cohesive-grade backbone is fully serial on those URNs: straighten (3) -> auto-tone (4) -> dark-portion lift (5) -> WB neutralize (6) -> vibrance (7) -> single preset (8) -> grain (9), each consuming the prior step's frames. From grained_frames (9) the chain branches: the graded kraft frame feeds image_fill_area (10 -> titlecard_charcoal_seated, seating the box on a solid charcoal #2B2B2B card by solid-fill only, within the existing near-square canvas); the graded jar frame feeds remove_background (11 -> jar_cutout_transparent) -> fill_area charcoal (12 -> jar_cutout_charcoal). Step 13 (crop_and_resize) reconverges 9+10+12 into the uniform 1920x1080 frame set, and this is where the near-square seated title card is cropped to the wide 1920x1080 title plate. A parallel stock branch fetches+licenses a plate (14) and resizes it (15). Quick Cut (16) consumes the frame set (13) + intro plate (15) to produce the hero reel; video_resize (17) consumes that reel to produce the square cut. Every step after the initial load consumes at least one prior output; the sizzle-reel deliverable emerges only at the end of the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 17 connector calls across 3 groups (asset/storage+stock, image photo-prep, video) with deep serial chaining and a branch/reconverge. 15 distinct Adobe tools (well over the >=6 / >=10 bar). It is genuinely hard because the brief reads as a 'video' task but the supplied assets are stills, so the agent must recognize that the honest Adobe path is a long cohesive-grade pipeline on the photos FIRST, then async video assembly — and must NOT reach for unavailable capabilities (no text-to-image B-roll, no compositing the products together, no AI object removal, no upscaling). the title card is produced honestly with no banned capability: image_fill_area (step 10) solid-fills the charcoal sweep to seat the box on a clean card within the existing canvas, then image_crop_and_resize (step 13) reframe-crops that near-square card to the wide 1920x1080 title plate — a pure crop of existing pixels, no canvas ever invented, padded, or extended. image_fill_area is used correctly as solid-color-only (charcoal card behind the jar cut-out and behind the kraft box, never a composite). The two video steps are honestly tagged [A] async-widget (product-doable, not headless-retrievable), and video_resize is used for a same-length reframe, never a trim. The cohesive-grade requirement across 6 deliberately mismatched source looks makes the ordering of WB-neutralize -> vibrance -> single shared preset -> uniform grain load-bearing, not arbitrary.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability anywhere. Step 10 is image_fill_area (solid charcoal #2B2B2B fill of the sweep surrounding the box, within the existing near-square kraft canvas, no canvas growth, no invented pixels, no compositing) producing titlecard_charcoal_seated; step 13 image_crop_and_resize reframe-crops that near-square seated card to the wide 1920x1080 title plate (titlecard_kraft_fullbleed) — a pure crop of existing pixels. All narrative fields (grounding_note, full_brief, kraft-box realism_notes/gen_prompt, chaining_note, difficulty_rationale) now match — no residual 'generative expand/upscale/from-scratch' phrasing remains. Remaining tools sweep clean: image_remove_background (11) is background-strip only; no text-to-image, no upscale, no object-removal-with-reconstruction. Both video steps stay honestly tagged [A]; video_resize is a same-length reframe. Chain stays fully connected (titlecard_charcoal_seated consumed by step 13; step 13's frame set consumed by step 16).
reverify.notes: tool_call_count is 17, distinct_adobe_tools 15 (the create_firefly_board sign-off moodboard step was trimmed as a low-value extra; image_fill_area and image_crop_and_resize both already appeared, so swapping step 10's tool does not change the distinct set). All seven client stills are now enumerated in inputs[] (six packaging photos + the kraft title card). The kraft source provides a generous charcoal surround so the solid-fill seated-card result is deterministic and gen-free.
